title: parquetina calophylla
intro: Parquetina calophylla, also known as the African plum tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is traditionally used in West African medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including the potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to reduce the risk of malaria. The herbal preparations of Parquetina calophylla are often made from the bark or leaves, and can be administered in various forms, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments.
